ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: M pátì kí fōō tiŋan tin a ŋá-mi nee nì tibulcin tin a dàkā man a-tɔntɔnliu nee, kun puee ŋkpandɔ baba ní m nìn ŋúb́ ki dūtì Jɔɔdan buŋŋu nee. Ama díǹ pu nee, m yākatì m-nib nì m-waŋkuti akiti ali ní nee.
translated: When I was a child, my mother and my grandmother told me stories about the great hunter who lived in the village of Jordan's pool, but now I have grown up and I tell my own stories to my children.

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ní u gíti cáá dipool ki màl̄ kibininjabiki, ní ki bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan nín ká yii m-cal kaa néeń-mee ní u gíti pú-mi kibininjabiki kiba.» Ní u pú-kì diyindi yii Simeeyɔn, taapu di sá yii: <Ti-Dindaan gbìl̄.>
translated: He said to the people of the village, this is what he said: "Ti-Dindaan has said that we should go and see the people of the village." He said this to Simeon, who then said: "Ti-Dindaan has died."

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Míǹ puee, yòon̄ a-tɔbu nì a-baatandi nì a-kpáŋwanti kí cù kukpɔŋu kí kpɔ̀ń-mi upeel.
translated: The child is crying, he fell on the stone and hit his head on the ground and got a wound on his forehead.

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Ní Abraam tóotì ki nín kóò u-tantiun u-nimpuu Saara cee, ní ki bí-u yii: «Malaa! Yáfí ŋyimmɔntiim kí búuń kí tɔ̄ kalaa!»
translated: Abraham fell on his face and laughed and said, "Will a child be born to a man one hundred years old? Will Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?"

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū ntam ní ki máń unil, ní ki púfì ŋfam ki ŋá u-maŋfataabuŋyin, ní u kpántì unifoo.
translated: The-Dindaan Unimbɔti went to the forest and saw a big tree, and saw a lot of fruit on the tree and the fruit was ripe, and he picked some fruit.
